In accordance with the common terminology used in professional service organisations, reference to a "partner" means a person who is a partner, or equivalent, in such a law firm. Similarly, reference to an "office" means an office of any such law firm. © 2013 Baker & McKenzie From construction to operation: what are the challenges for New Caledonian projects? 5th New Caledonia Nickel Conference 1 -5 July 2013 - Le Meridien Noumea Mathieu Hanaut Partner – Baker & McKenzie Sydney - Australia © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Introduction Key facts and figures (source ADECAL) – 18,500 Km2 – GDP 9 billion USD (2008) – GDP per Capita 36.175 USD (Australia 50,746 USD and New Zealand 32,620 USD) – Population 252,000 (2011)  Melanesians 44%  Europeans 34%  Polynesians 12%  Asians 4%  Other 6% – Language: French – Currency: French Pacific Franc (XPF) Fixed rate with Euro (sources: ADECAL) 2

chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Construction regulations – Liability of the main contractor – Obligations of the main contractor when sub- contracting 11 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Liability of the main contractor – French regulation imposes 10 years liability on certain construction work – Application of this 10 year building contractor liability in New Caledonia? Implicit application? – Main criteria: the type of the construction (building, industrial infrastructure, other) – Insurance issues – When contracting in an international context which law is applicable? 12 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Obligation of the main contractor when sub- contracting – Security of payment obligation of the main contractor toward the sub-contractor – Provide a Bank guarantee to secure payment – Mandatory statutory regulation (Law dated 31/01/1975 – applicable in NC since 1997!) – Failure to provide the bank guarantee may invalidate the sub- contract agreement – Substantial risk if lump sum contract 13

chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Nickel projects landscape Dramatic changes over the past 10 years – 3 majors players invested in excess of 12 billion USD in mining and metalurgical projects construction:  Eramet - SLN  GlencoreXstrata - KNS  Vale – Vale NC – New Caledonia should become the third largest producer of Nickel Metal in the world by 2020 3 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Legal perspective: from construction to operation what are the challenges? – Brief overview of the transfer of power mechanism – Construction – Operation – Conclusion 4 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Sophisticated Regulations – Self determination process  Nouméa Agreement of May 1998  Organic Law of March 1999 – Powers progressively transferred to New Caledonia – Civil (French Law) legal system – Safe legal framework – Transitional adjustments 5 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Heads of powers progressively transferred to New Caledonia Transferred to New Caledonia – Taxation, Labour law, Access to work for foreigners, Native title, Insurance, Mining Remaining with the French State – Monetary Policy, Justice, Education, Immigration, Police, Defence Force On-going Transfer to New Caledonia – Education, Civil and Commercial Law 6

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p7::c0
chunk.doc_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию
chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie I – Construction © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Construction Phase – Employing people – Construction regulations 8 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Employing people during construction phase – Labour law is a head of power transferred to New Caledonia – In 2010, a New Caledonian Labour Law Code was passed – Identical to the pre-existing New Caledonian regulation – Largely inspired by French law: Protective of employees – Special regime dealing with Employment of Foreign Employees in the context of mining projects construction 9 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Special regime applying to foreign employees assigned to New Caledonia by a foreign employer – Conditions of Employment (New Caledonian law)  Specific regime – NC law 2002 (Loi PSI) article LP 621-1  This regime is slightly more favorable for Employers  Facilitation of overhours authorisation up to 60 hours per week (subject to conditions)  Work permit facilitation – Only apply to foreign employees, assigned in New Caledonia by a foreign employer for the construction of mining or metallurgical projects or for works modifying the structure or infrastructure of mining or metallurgical activities. 10

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p14::c0
chunk.doc_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию
chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie II – Operation © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Operation Phase – Litigation – Arbitration - Mediation – Employing people – Taxation 15 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Litigation – Arbitration - Mediation – Construction Completion / Commissioning are favourable timing for litigation – Start of Operation phase frequently = Litigation – In New Caledonia for the largest contract – Arbitration – What about local New Caledonian jurisdiction ? – Mediation  Legal costs savings and costs sharing approach  Short time frame  Cultural and language facilitation  Commercial solution  Confidentiality 16 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Employing people during operation phase – We have seen that the special regime applies to employees, assigned in New Caledonia by a foreign employer for the construction of mining or metallurgical projects or for works modifying the structure or infrastructure of mining or metallurgical activities.

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p20::c0
chunk.doc_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию
chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Protection of the Local Employment 5 fundamental principles of the August 2010 Act – Positive discrimination to favor employment of New Caledonian citizen or long term resident (Priority person) – Procedure: jobs must be advertised for a set period of time, if no Priority persons qualified for the job, then a foreign employee may be engaged – A monitoring body will advise on any litigious employment – A table listing existing professional activities and shortage in Priority person has been approved by Arrete (9/01/2012). – Penalties in case of breach and termination of the employee wrongfully engaged Suggestion: Exemption of this procedure for companies providing training (Similar to the Australian business immigration system) 20 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Pros/Cons on Employer’s Perspective 21 GENERAL REGIME (Operation) SPECIFIC REGIME (Construction) VISA /WORK PERMIT + - CAFAT (SOCIAL CONTRIBUTION*) - + PRIVATE HEALTH INSURANCE + - CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT - + FLEXIBILITY OF THE CONTRACT - + LONG TERM EMPLOYMENT + + WORKING HOURS + + *Exemption not based on article LP 621-2 of the NC Labour Code but on NC Law 11/01/2002 regarding social protection

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p14::c1
chunk.doc_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию
chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: or the construction of mining or metallurgical projects or for works modifying the structure or infrastructure of mining or metallurgical activities. – Consequence: at the end of the construction phase, the General regime applies: – Conditions of Employment is the General regime applicable in New Caledonia:  working hours: maximum of 10 hours daily, 40 hours weekly and 169 hours monthly;  Territorial or Industrial awards apply;  mandatory termination procedures. – 17

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p18::c0
chunk.doc_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию
chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie How is “construction phase” defined? – No legal or regulatory definition; – Discretion and flexibility of the Government in its determination of the construction phase; – Goro Project is a good example:  the end of the construction phase was set at June 2011 subject to exceptions;  First exception: for the Kwe Bassin, “construction” was extended until December 2011;  Second exception: companies involved in the ramp up benefited from an exemption until December 2011. 18 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Employing people during operation phase – However the regulation indicates “works modifying the structure or infrastructure of mining or metallurgical activities” → fall under the Special Regime. – No definition of “works modifying the structure or infrastructure of mining or metallurgical activities” and no precedent. → Discretion of the Government. – It it likely that the following fall into the definition:  extension works;  maintenance or repair works of significant importance. 19

chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Taxation – Various favourable regime end with the operation phase. – Tax definition of the operation phase or the end of the “Construction phase”: 80% of the production reach during 3 consecutive months  Start of the computation of the tax holidays period  Stop of the importation tax exemption special regime 22 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie III – Conclusion © 2013 Baker & McKenzie NC Employment check list – Immigration/ work permit procedure  – Local employment or assignment?  – Compliant contracts  – Project contract rather than fixed term contract  – Compliant working hours  – Did you lodge an exemption to work up to 60h/week?  – Labour hiring = legal risks  – Mandatory termination procedure  – Get a good lawyer  24 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Construction check list – What is the nature of the construction (Building / Industrial) ?  – Is their any mandatory construction liability applicable ?  – Insurance  – Security of payment for Sub-Contractors – Bank guarantee in place  – Mediation  25

chunk.chunk_id: Материалы конференций__Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию::p26::c0

chunk.source: /content/drive/MyDrive/minekg/raw/Материалы конференций/Mathieu_Hanaut_Реализация проекта от строительства до ввода в эксплуатацию.pdf
chunk.text: © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Mining Regulation Check List – Dimenc visit and continuing communication  – Relationships with stakeholders (mining bodies, Provinces, Native and local communities)  – Think permitting and authorisation far in advance  – Compliance with the new regulation/authorisation for existing mines  – Compliance with due diligence before investment/acquisition  – Authorisation/permits/approval will be transaction CPs when necessary  – Appropriate vendor warranties and indemnities in place  26 © 2013 Baker & McKenzie Environmental check list – Permiting for Classified Installations involved in environmental and mining projects   Prepare declaration or authorisation application files (in particular, Environmental Impact Assessment and Danger Risk Assessment)   Get ready for public enquiry procedure  – Continuing communication with DIMENC and Provinces  – Check compliance with other environmental regulations (water, waste, air, biodiversity…)  – Check compliance due diligence before investment/acquisition  – Negotiation of appropriate warranties and indemnities  27
